# signal CLI 名前空間統一と gate strictness の宣言的管理

## Tasks (12/19 resolved)

### S1 — Domain Foundation: Chain Types, Traits, and Blanket Impl (D7)

> Establish the Rust type foundation in `libs/domain/src/chain.rs`: the four chain value-object types (ChainId, Strictness, GateKind, ChainGateEntry, SignalGateMatrix), the four trait definitions (ChainIdentity, SoTChain, LiveSoTChain, PersistedSoTChain), and the `impl<T: PersistedSoTChain> SoTChain for T` blanket impl.
> Extract the chain ② Red/Yellow gate logic into `domain::check_catalogue_spec_signals` — a pure domain function symmetric with the existing chain ① and ③ pure functions.

- [x] **T001**: D7: Add `libs/domain/src/chain.rs` (new file) with: `ChainId` enum (AdrUser/SpecAdr/CatalogSpec/ImplCatalog), `Strictness` enum (Strict/Interim), `GateKind` enum (Commit/Merge), `ChainGateEntry` struct with `commit_gate: Strictness` + `merge_gate: Strictness` + `resolve(GateKind) -> Strictness` method, `SignalGateMatrix` struct with four `ChainGateEntry` fields + `resolve(ChainId, GateKind) -> Strictness` method, and four trait definitions: `ChainIdentity` (associated const ID + GAT Input), `SoTChain: ChainIdentity` (associated fn `check`), `LiveSoTChain: SoTChain` (associated fn `calc_live`), `PersistedSoTChain: ChainIdentity` (associated fns `calc`, `load`, `check_freshness`, `evaluate_gate`, `calc_error`, `stale_error`), plus `impl<T: PersistedSoTChain> SoTChain for T` blanket impl. Re-export types from `libs/domain/src/lib.rs`. Standard derives: Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq for all enums; Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq for structs. No serde on domain types (serde-free constraint). Unit tests: ChainGateEntry::resolve returns correct Strictness for each GateKind variant; SignalGateMatrix::resolve returns correct cell for each (ChainId, GateKind) pair. Estimated diff: ~200 lines. (`858ff46e7ba4fe4fb7613b4a07cdf3738664d9f7`)
- [x] **T002**: D2 / CN-05: Add `domain::chain::check_catalogue_spec_signals(doc: &CatalogueSpecSignalsDocument, strict: bool) -> VerifyOutcome` pure domain free function in `libs/domain/src/chain.rs` (or a sub-module `libs/domain/src/chain/check.rs` re-exported from `chain`). This function extracts the Red/Yellow gate logic currently duplicated in `libs/usecase/src/merge_gate/chain2_gate.rs` (inline) and `libs/infrastructure/src/verify/catalogue_spec_signals.rs` (inline) into a single domain-layer pure function, symmetric with the existing `check_spec_doc_signals` (chain ①) and `check_type_signals` (chain ③). Rules: Red signals always `Finding::error` regardless of `strict`; Yellow signals → `Finding::error` when `strict=true`, `Finding::warning` when `strict=false`. Export from `libs/domain/src/lib.rs`. Unit tests: all-blue doc passes; red signal errors regardless of strict; yellow strict=true errors; yellow strict=false warns. Estimated diff: ~100 lines. (`bd7c28ca06d0a72bc909c7fb323194cbce39c839`)

### S3 — Usecase Chain Structs and Trait Impls (D7)

> Implement the four chain structs in `libs/usecase/src/chain/`: AdrUserChain (LiveSoTChain, no persistence), SpecAdrChain, CatalogSpecChain, ImplCatalogChain (all PersistedSoTChain, gaining SoTChain via blanket impl). Each struct wires its evaluate_gate to the appropriate domain pure function and its check_freshness to the appropriate hash comparison mechanism.

- [x] **T005**: D7: Add `libs/usecase/src/chain/` directory with four sub-modules: `adr_user.rs` (AdrUserChain unit struct implementing ChainIdentity + SoTChain + LiveSoTChain — live scan of knowledge/adr/ frontmatter, no persistence file); `spec_adr.rs` (SpecAdrChain unit struct implementing ChainIdentity + PersistedSoTChain — `evaluate_gate` delegates to `domain::check_spec_doc_signals`, `check_freshness` implements self-consistency check comparing `doc.evaluate_signals()` vs `doc.signals()`); `catalog_spec.rs` (CatalogSpecChain unit struct implementing ChainIdentity + PersistedSoTChain — `evaluate_gate` delegates to `domain::check_catalogue_spec_signals` (T002), `check_freshness` compares entry_hash from signals doc with current catalogue bytes SHA-256); `impl_catalog.rs` (ImplCatalogChain unit struct implementing ChainIdentity + PersistedSoTChain — `evaluate_gate` delegates to `domain::check_type_signals`, `check_freshness` compares declaration_hash from type-signals doc with current catalogue bytes SHA-256). Add `mod.rs` to re-export all four. All four get SoTChain via blanket impl from T001 (chains ①②③) or direct impl (chain ⓪). Unit/static trait-bound tests: assert AdrUserChain satisfies ChainIdentity + SoTChain + LiveSoTChain, assert SpecAdrChain/CatalogSpecChain/ImplCatalogChain satisfy ChainIdentity + PersistedSoTChain and are accepted by SoTChain-bound helper functions through the blanket impl, and cover freshness/evaluate_gate delegation happy/error paths with mocked persisted documents. Depends on T001 and T002. Estimated diff: ~350 lines. (`2b0c636ebfe8ed73308f072f5cb0b6f83f105497`)

### S8 — Parser Extension + Formatter Dedup + Catalogue Schema + Canonical Declarations + doc(hidden) Removal (ADR 2026-06-18-0822 D1/D2/D3/D4)

> Six tasks bring the trait taxonomy to 🔵 Blue across parser, formatter, domain schema, catalogue plan-artifact, and the blanket-impl visibility fix.
> T014 (parser D1, DONE/committed 12ab1161): Replace the `qself.is_some()` early-return stub with a full `Type::QualifiedPath` builder. UNLOCKS expressiveness only — GAT-related Yellows remain because the catalogue still uses `"Self"` placeholder.
> T015 (parser D2, DONE/committed 55572c70): Add `generic_params: &[&str]` context to `parse_type_ref_str` and insert a `Type::Generic(name)` step after `resolve_local`. UNLOCKS expressiveness only.
> T016 (D3 formatter dedup, implementer): Dedup the two QualifiedPath inline arms in `format_type_common_arms` / `fmt_rec` → single `format_qualified_path_with` delegation. Normalize empty-path Self-projection (`trait_=Some(Path{path:""})`) to `"_"` token, converging C-side and A-side to the same string.
> T017 (D4 catalogue schema, domain+infra): Add `assoc_types: Vec<AssocTypeDecl>` and `assoc_consts: Vec<AssocConstDecl>` to `TraitEntry`. Extend infra codecs to emit `ItemEnum::AssocType` / `ItemEnum::AssocConst`. Backward-compat: missing fields default to empty. OS-09/OS-10 explicitly NOT done.
> T018 (D3c catalogue declarations, plan-artifact): Update `domain-types.json`: (a) revise GAT params[].ty from `"Self"` placeholder to `<Self>::Assoc<'_>` canonical form; (b) add assoc_types / assoc_consts declarations to the four trait entries; (c) confirm blanket impl entry with impl_generics+impl_where_predicates.
> T019 (blanket-impl doc(hidden) removal + final signal check, domain): Strip `#[doc(hidden)]` from the blanket impl in `libs/domain/src/chain.rs`. Final verification: domain-layer signals all Blue (CI-verified 46 Blue / 0 Yellow / 0 Red).
> EXECUTION ORDER: T014 and T015 are committed. T016 (formatter), T017 (schema), T018 (declarations) are implemented in the spike worktree. T019 follows T016+T017+T018. T016/T017/T018/T019 are implemented but uncommitted; commit in the order: T016+T017 together (infra+domain code), then T018 (plan-artifact), then T019 (domain code + final signal check).

- [x] **T014**: Parser D1 (ADR 2026-06-18-0822 §D1) — `Type::QualifiedPath` builder: Replace the early-return `unresolved_type("<qualified_path>")` branch at `libs/infrastructure/src/tddd/type_ref_parser/parse_ctx.rs` line 60-63 with a builder that constructs `rustdoc_types::Type::QualifiedPath { name, self_type, trait_, args }`. Use `convert_type` recursively on `qself.ty` for `self_type`, `resolve_trait_bound_path` on the path segments before `qself.position` for `trait_`, the immediately-following segment ident for `name`, and `convert_generic_args` on that segment's generic arguments for `args`. Do not touch `format_qualified_path_with`, `methods_structurally_equal`, or the catalogue schema. Add unit tests in the same module covering: simple `<T as Trait>::Assoc` without args, canonical GAT projection `&<Self as ChainIdentity>::Input<'_>`, and qualified-path inside Vec args `Vec<<T as Trait>::Item>`. The production catalogue still uses `"Self"` placeholder in `params[].ty`; the existing 4 GAT-related Yellows (`ChainIdentity` / `SoTChain` / `LiveSoTChain` / `PersistedSoTChain`) REMAIN Yellow after this task. Acceptance: `bin/sotp track type-signals` continues to exit 0 (no regression) and the new unit tests pass. Estimated diff: ~60 lines (net addition replaces 4-line stub). Depends on T005. spec_anchors: IN-11, CN-10. (`12ab11610ac160948f5be747ee45d3d67f8b37a5`)
- [x] **T015**: Parser D2 (ADR 2026-06-18-0822 §D2) — `generic_params` context + `Type::Generic` encoding: Add `generic_params: &[&str]` parameter to `parse_type_ref_str` (and propagate through all reachable internal parser entry points). In `convert_type_path`, insert a new step after `resolve_local` (and before the Unresolved fallback): if the single-segment identifier is found in `generic_params`, return `Type::Generic(name)` immediately. Update all infrastructure-side codec callsites that decode `TraitImplDeclV2.for_type` to compute a `generic_params` slice from the entry's `impl_generics: Vec<MethodGenericParam>` names and pass it to the parser. For method signatures, propagate a `generic_params` context derived from trait generics + method generics through `methods[].params[].ty` and `methods[].returns` decoding so that generic-param names are resolved consistently throughout a method signature. Add unit tests using a smoke-test fixture: `for_type: "T"` with `impl_generics: [{name: "T"}]` → `Type::Generic("T")`; `for_type: "T"` with no `impl_generics` → falls through to `Type::ResolvedPath` (existing behaviour preserved). SIGNAL IMPACT: This task unlocks parser expressiveness only. No production catalogue entry with `for_type: "T"` + `impl_generics` exists yet; `bin/sotp track type-signals` continues to exit 0 (no regression) after this task. The fixture-level smoke test verifies the new code path independently of the catalogue. The actual catalogue blanket impl entry is NOT added until T016. Acceptance: `bin/sotp track type-signals` exits 0; smoke-test unit tests pass. Estimated diff: ~120 lines. Independent of T014. spec_anchors: IN-12, CN-11. - [x] **T016**: D3 Formatter dedup (ADR 2026-06-18-0822 §D3): In `libs/infrastructure/src/tddd/signal_evaluator_v2/format/ty_base.rs`, remove the two duplicated QualifiedPath inline arms from `format_type_common_arms` and `fmt_rec`, delegating both to the shared `format_qualified_path_with` helper. Also normalize the empty-path Self-projection trait: in `format_qualified_path_with`, treat `trait_` = `Some(Path { path: "" })` the same as `None`, outputting `"_"` in both cases. This makes the C-side empty-path Self projection and the A-side `<Self>::Assoc<args>` catalogue literal (parsed as `trait_=None`) converge to the comparison string `<Self as _>::Assoc<args>`, enabling string-equality comparison to pass. Acceptance: `bin/sotp track type-signals` exits 0 (no regression); unit/integration tests demonstrate the DRY dedup and empty-path normalization. spec_anchors: IN-13, CN-13, AC-17.
- [x] **T017**: D4 Catalogue schema extension (ADR 2026-06-18-0822 §D4): Add `assoc_types: Vec<AssocTypeDecl>` and `assoc_consts: Vec<AssocConstDecl>` fields to `TraitEntry` in `libs/domain/src/tddd/catalogue_v2/entries.rs`. `AssocTypeDecl` holds `name: TypeName` / `bounds: Vec<TypeRef>` / `default: Option<TypeRef>`; `AssocConstDecl` holds `name: AssocConstName` / `ty: TypeRef` / `default_value: Option<String>`, with `AssocConstName` added as a validated newtype for associated const identifiers. Both fields default to empty (`#[serde(default)]`) to maintain backward compat with existing TraitEntry JSON, and optional default fields are omitted when `None`. Extend the infrastructure codecs `catalogue_document_codec` and `encoder_state_fn_trait_codec` to emit `ItemEnum::AssocType` / `ItemEnum::AssocConst` items. GAT lifetime / type params are intentionally excluded from `AssocTypeDecl` per OS-09. Do NOT relax `items.len` comparison in `trait_eq.rs` per OS-10. spec_anchors: IN-14, CN-15, OS-09, OS-10, AC-18.

### S4 — Infrastructure Verify Rewiring: strict params, Stage1/Stage2 split, domain delegation (D2)

> Add `strict: bool` to `execute_verify_adr_signals`. Add `read_adr_verify_report` to `TrackBlobReader`. Wire chain ⓪ into `check_strict_merge_gate`.
> Split `verify_from_spec_json` into Stage1 (check-spec-adr with self-consistency freshness check) and separate Stage2 (check-impl-catalog with independent strict).
> Replace inline Red/Yellow logic in `execute_catalogue_spec_signals*` and `chain2_gate.rs` with calls to `domain::check_catalogue_spec_signals`.

- [x] **T006**: D2 / D5: (1) Modify `libs/infrastructure/src/verify/adr_signals.rs::execute_verify_adr_signals` to accept `strict: bool` parameter — when `strict=true`, `ReviewFindingRef` (Yellow) promotes to `Finding::error`; Red (`NoGrounds`) always errors; `Grandfathered` always skip. (2) Add `read_adr_verify_report(&self, branch: String) -> BlobFetchResult<domain::AdrVerifyReport>` method to the `TrackBlobReader` trait in `libs/usecase/src/merge_gate.rs`, with a corresponding adapter impl that scans ADR frontmatter for the target branch and returns the domain report without exposing a filesystem path to the usecase layer. (3) Modify the merge-gate caller at the CLI/infrastructure boundary to load `SignalGateMatrix` via `load_signal_gates_config` and pass the validated domain matrix into `usecase::merge_gate::check_strict_merge_gate`; the usecase function itself must not depend on infrastructure. (4) Modify `check_strict_merge_gate` to accept that `domain::SignalGateMatrix`, pass its existing `branch` argument to `reader.read_adr_verify_report(branch)`, resolve `GateKind::Merge` strictness for all four cells (`adr_user`, `spec_adr`, `catalog_spec`, `impl_catalog`), use the resolved strict bools for the existing chain ①②③ evaluations instead of hardcoded `strict=true`, and add chain ⓪ evaluation via `AdrUserChain::check` (or equivalent report-to-outcome path) with the resolved `merge_gate.adr_user` strictness. (2) `execute_catalogue_spec_signals_check` — same delegation. Also remove the corresponding inline logic from `libs/usecase/src/merge_gate/chain2_gate.rs::check_chain2_for_layer` and replace with a call to `domain::check_catalogue_spec_signals`. ### S5 — CLI signal Namespace: 8 Commands + Aggregate check (D1 / D5)

> Add `apps/cli/src/commands/signal/` with the 8 orthogonal commands (`calc-*` / `check-*` × 4 chains) and the aggregate `signal check --gate commit|merge`. CLI surface uses `catalog` spelling. Each `check-*` resolves strictness from `--strict` or from `load_signal_gates_config` when `--gate` is specified.

- [ ] **T009**: D1 / D5 / AC-01 / AC-11: Add `apps/cli/src/commands/signal/` module with 8 individual commands and 1 aggregate: `calc_adr_user.rs` (delegates to AdrUserChain::calc_live via LiveSoTChain, no-persist, live display); `check_adr_user.rs` (delegates to AdrUserChain::check via SoTChain, resolves strictness from --strict flag or --gate + load_signal_gates_config); `calc_spec_adr.rs` (delegates to SpecAdrChain::calc via PersistedSoTChain, persists signals to spec.json); `check_spec_adr.rs` (delegates to SpecAdrChain::check via SoTChain with freshness, resolves strictness); `calc_catalog_spec.rs` (delegates to CatalogSpecChain::calc); `check_catalog_spec.rs` (delegates to CatalogSpecChain::check with entry_hash freshness); `calc_impl_catalog.rs` (delegates to ImplCatalogChain::calc); `check_impl_catalog.rs` (delegates to ImplCatalogChain::check with declaration_hash freshness); `mod.rs` with SignalCommand enum routing all 8 sub-commands plus aggregate `check --gate commit|merge`. Add `signal` to the top-level sotp command enum. UI uses `catalog` spelling (not `catalogue`). Each `check-*` accepts `--strict` override and `--gate commit|merge` for config-driven resolution. The aggregate `signal check --gate` runs all 4 chains in order and aggregates findings. Tests: parser/dispatch tests for all 8 commands and aggregate `signal check --gate`, strict-vs-gate resolution tests (including invalid flag combinations if mutually exclusive), config-driven strictness cell selection for commit/merge, aggregate all-four-chain execution with mocked outcomes, and CLI surface assertions that command names use `catalog` spelling and no old command aliases remain. Depends on T001, T003, T005, T006, T007, T008. Estimated diff: ~420 lines.

### S7 — Config Validation Tests (D4)

> Comprehensive unit tests for `load_signal_gates_config` covering all hard-error branches: file not found, parse failure, schema version unknown, missing key, invalid value. Integration test that the committed default config parses to the expected SignalGateMatrix.

- [ ] **T013**: D4 / AC-05: Add comprehensive unit tests for `load_signal_gates_config` covering all hard-error scenarios: (1) file not found → `SignalGatesConfigError::FileNotFound`; (2) malformed JSON → `ParseFailed`; (3) `$schema_version` = 0 or 99 → `SchemaVersionUnknown`; (4) missing `commit_gate` object → `MissingKey`; (5) missing individual chain×gate cell → `MissingKey`; (6) invalid strictness string (e.g. `"permissive"`) → `InvalidValue`; (7) valid config with recommended defaults → Ok with expected `SignalGateMatrix`; (8) valid config with all-strict → Ok. Also add integration-level test verifying that the committed `.harness/config/signal-gates.json` (T004) parses successfully. Add unit tests for `SignalGatesConfigError::Display` variants (actionable messages). Depends on T003, T004. Estimated diff: ~200 lines.
